Question 2:
Access and name at least one Australian corporate sustainability standards used by your organisation/workplace. 
Review this document and complete the table below. 
a.	Identify at least two guidance or requirement under this standard that your organisation can implement towards sustainability practices.
Indicate the clause or specific section within the document where you accessed each guidance or requirement.
b.	For each guidance or requirement, briefly describe an example of how your organisation can demonstrate its implementation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: As an AI language model, I do not have access to your specific organisation or workplace to identify the Australian corporate sustainability standards used by them, and therefore cannot complete the table. However, based on research, some examples of Australian sustainability standards used by organisations include the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI), ISO 14001 (Environmental Management Systems), and the Carbon Neutral Certification standard. It is recommended to consult with your organisation to access information about the specific standards they use.

Question 4:
Complete the table below. 
a.	Identify sources of information that can be used when planning and developing sustainability policies and procedures for the workplace/organisation.
        i. At least two must be internal sources of information 
        ii. At least two must be external sources of information
b.	For each source identified, in your own words, briefly describe how they are used when planning and developing sustainability policies and procedures.
Answer:
Internal Sources of Information	How It is Used in Planning and Developing Sustainability Policies and Procedures
a.	     	●	     
b.	     	●	     

External Sources of Information	How It is Used in Planning and Developing Sustainability Policies and Procedures
a.	     	●	     
b.	     	●

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Internal Sources of Information	How It is Used in Planning and Developing Sustainability Policies and Procedures
a. Existing Sustainability Policies and Procedures	●	Provide a starting point for reviewing, updating, or developing new sustainability policies and procedures that align with the organisation's current goals and priorities.
b. Employee Feedback and Suggestions	●	Involve employees in the sustainability planning process, incorporating their ideas and concerns to ensure policies and procedures are practical and effective.

External Sources of Information	How It is Used in Planning and Developing Sustainability Policies and Procedures
a. Regulatory Requirements	●	Provide guidance on legal obligations and compliance in sustainability practices, ensuring that the organisation's policies and procedures align with industry standards and regulations.
b. Industry Best Practices and Standards	●	Provide benchmarking for sustainability strategies and policies that have been successful in similar industries or organisations, aiding in the development of effective and innovative sustainability policies and procedures.
